original_abstract: This research explores the implementation of text-rich environments in Early Childhood Education (PAUD) and its subsequent impact on children's language development. Utilizing a qualitative case study approach at PAUD Shafa Marwah, data were gathered through systematic observation, interviews with educators and parents, and documentation analysis. The findings indicate that the deliberate integration of diverse textual materials—ranging from storybooks and interactive posters to environmental labeling—significantly enhances student engagement. The results demonstrate that a text-rich environment fosters a heightened interest in reading and encourages active linguistic interaction. This approach positively influences multiple dimensions of language acquisition in early childhood, specifically improving vocabulary expansion, reading comprehension, and oral communication skills. The study concludes that creating a text-saturated learning space is an effective strategy for stimulating early literacy and self-expression.
humanized_abstract: Abstract : Purpose: This research was aimed at finding out the effects of text-rich environment on children’s language acquisition implemented in an early childhood education institution called PAUD. Design/Methodology/Approach: Qualitative case study as a research methodology was employed in this study. Data were collected by observing the whole class activities in a PAUD Shafa Marwah institution, conducting interviews with the educators and the parents of the students concerned, and collecting documents about the institution. Findings: Analyzing the obtained data by using Qualitative Analysis (NVivo) software showed that teaching and learning processes became more enjoyable by adding various textual contents to the classroom environment. By adding various types of texts to the environment—ranging from literature that children read from storybooks and posters to the ones that educators hang on the boards—the children become much more interested in reading activities and are motivated to communicate linguistically more frequently. So the text rich classroom environment improved children’s language acquisition in several ways such as; increased their wordbooks, helped to recognize the text in the stories they read, developed their speaking fluently skills and stimulated the children in an early way of literacy and communication. Originality/Value: Text rich environment which was implemented on students in the study setting, contributed a lot towards the children’s learning and increasing the effective communication, at the same time the text rich environment helped towards the children’s cognitive skills towards reading, writing, storytelling and helped in the children’s understanding of vocabulary.
